content: read reviews/201.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/201.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/201.md.

    35‚Üí- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; N/A for `local notation`.
    36‚Üí- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks on definitions. **Minor observation** ‚ÄĒ optional per checklist ("Consider adding").
    37‚Üí

    40‚Üí- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 79).
    41‚Üí- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_201` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    42‚Üí- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]` (line 78).
    43‚Üí- [x] Has a docstring (lines 69‚Äď77).

    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí### `answer()` usage
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíThe English problem asks *"Is it true that $\lim_{N\to\infty} R_3(N)/G_3(N) = 1$?"* ‚ÄĒ this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    49‚Üí

    51‚Üí- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (for all őĶ > 0, for sufficiently large N, every set of N integers has a 3-AP-free subset of size ‚Č• (1 ‚ąí őĶ)¬∑R‚āÉ(N)), so `answer(True)` would mean "yes".

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/201.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19‚Äď47).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 201`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/201](https://www.erdosproblems.com/201)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines $G_k(N)$, $R_k(N)$, states the trivial bound and the Koml√≥s‚ÄďSulyok‚ÄďSzemer√©di result).
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [Er73], [Er75b], [ErGr79], [ErGr80], [KSS75].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace: `namespace Erdos201 ... end Erdos201` (lines 51‚Äď87).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos201`.
- [x] `open Classical` appears **before** the namespace block (line 49).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `HasKTermAP` (line 58): has a LaTeX docstring (lines 53‚Äď57).
- [x] `maxAPFreeSize` (line 62): has a LaTeX docstring (line 61). Marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] `rAp` (line 66): has a LaTeX docstring (line 65). Marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `HasKTermAP` (returns `Prop`).
- [x] Functions returning values use `lowerCamelCase`: `maxAPFreeSize`, `rAp`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; N/A for `local notation`.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks on definitions. **Minor observation** ‚ÄĒ optional per checklist ("Consider adding").

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 79).
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_201` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]` (line 78).
- [x] Has a docstring (lines 69‚Äď77).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er73, Er75b, ErGr79, ErGr80]`.

### `answer()` usage

The English problem asks *"Is it true that $\lim_{N\to\infty} R_3(N)/G_3(N) = 1$?"* ‚ÄĒ this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` (lines 80‚Äď84).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (for all őĶ > 0, for sufficiently large N, every set of N integers has a 3-AP-free subset of size ‚Č• (1 ‚ąí őĶ)¬∑R‚āÉ(N)), so `answer(True)` would mean "yes".
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚ąÄ őĶ`, `‚ąÉ N‚āÄ`, `‚ąÄ N`, `‚ąÄ S`, `‚ąÉ T`) are **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS of `‚ÜĒ`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
- [x] Research open theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` format (`:= by\n  sorry` on lines 84‚Äď85). Multi-line proof with `sorry` is acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present. The file formalizes a single conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąą`, `‚äÜ`, `¬¨`, `‚ÜĒ`, `‚Üí`, `‚Č§`, `‚Č†`, `‚Ąē`, `‚Ą§`, `‚ĄĚ`, `őĶ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. Must be verified separately.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified during this review.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS (modulo build verification)**

The file is clean and fully conformant with all stylistic, structural, and semantic requirements of the checklist. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question (Case 1) and uses `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` accordingly. Definitions are well-documented with docstrings, appropriately marked `noncomputable`, and follow naming conventions. The only unchecked items are the optional sanity-check attributes on definitions and the build verification step which requires running `lake build`.

content.text: The review status is **PASS**. No changes needed.
